title: Intense Bitter Turkish Leaf Icli Kofte
description: A nut-free, bouncy Turkish leaf stuffed meatball with an intense bitter flavor, perfect for a brunch‑mood bento box.
instructions: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and blanch the Chinese cabbage leaves for 1 minute, then shock them in ice water and set aside.
In a bowl combine the ground lamb, ground beef, onion, garlic, parsley, mint, cumin, smoked paprika, salt and pepper until well mixed.
Warm a skillet over medium heat, add sunflower oil, and cook the meat mixture for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it starts to brown.
Transfer the cooked meat mixture back into the bowl and let it cool slightly to avoid hardening the leaves.
Take a blanched leaf, place a generous spoonful of the meat mixture in the center, fold the sides over and roll tightly to form a 4‑5 inch long individual kotte.
Arrange the rolled kotte in a single layer inside a bento box, leaving space for the sauce.
In a small saucepan, heat 200ml water, add a pinch of salt, and bring to a gentle simmer.
Place the kotte in the simmering water, cover, and cook for 20 minutes, turning once, until the meat is fully cooked and the leaf remains tender.
Remove the kotte from the water, drain, and let them cool to room temperature before packing.
Serve the bitter, bouncy kotte chilled or at room temperature in the bento box, optionally with a side of yogurt dip.
